response.justification: The candidate is an experienced polyglot engineer with strong testing culture and low-latency systems background, but he fails every mandatory requirement of this role: his only professional C++ experience is a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (versus the required 5-10 years on Windows), his database experience is MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a retail investment-setup startup, not capital markets. The gap between his profile (web/TypeScript/Node/DLang) and this C++ fixed-income role is too large to bridge in an interview.
strengths: Broad, versatile engineering background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, caching)
Solid DevOps and tooling experience (GitLab, CI servers, Docker) plus fluent English and demonstrated communication skills through workshops and talks
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience (National Instruments, 2014-2015), far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++
No SQL Server or Oracle experience — his database work is MySQL and MongoDB
No fixed-income, derivatives, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience, and no Summit platform exposure

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and CI/CD fundamentals
